title: 09 meg has not turned around yet
prompt: Use case: ads-marketing. REFERENCE AUTHORITY — Image 1 locks Meg. Image 2 locks the top-row world. Image 3 supplies only the distant anime chaos affecting the game. BEAT — Tight shallow-focus portrait of Meg calmly examining one peeled deep-purple grape gummy in her fingertips. She has not turned around yet. Far below behind her, the referee freezes mid-whistle inside a graphic burst while players trail orange speed lines and the crowd reacts. The gag is the contrast between her quiet concentration and obvious distant chaos. No grape creature is visible yet. AVOID — bored contempt, looking at camera, green gummy, creature already emerged, crowded top row, whole frame becoming animation.

resolved_params.system_prompt: Create one polished cinematic live-action Peelerz commercial styleframe, never a collage, contact sheet, or storyboard grid. Treat each attached image only according to its declared authority. Preserve Raul and Meg as the same teenagers, and preserve the established top-of-bleachers world, dusk light, clothing, and geography. Attached ingredient sheets are exact character or object design authorities; reproduce them faithfully rather than redesigning them from prose. Keep the surreal event localized and legible inside the live-action stadium. Candy remains tactile, translucent, appetizing, and benign. No anatomy, gore, generic gummy bears, captions, borders, watermarks, or invented package copy.
